title: Monostable Circuit (Pulse Shortener)
description: Converts a long held signal into a brief pulse. The input passes through a repeater before reaching a torch's suppressor block — the torch fires immediately when the input arrives but the repeater delays the turn-off signal, so the output is ON for only (input_duration − repeater_delay) ticks. If the input is shorter than the repeater delay, no pulse is emitted at all. Set the repeater to delay 1–4 to control minimum pulse length.

steps.title: Stone base
steps.explanation: Five stone blocks in a line. The signal travels left to right: input lever → dust → repeater → dust → torch base.

steps.title: Input lever and dust
steps.explanation: Lever at (0,1,0) is the signal source. Dust at (1,1,0) and (2,1,0) carries the signal east to the repeater's input.

steps.title: Repeater
steps.explanation: Place the repeater at (3,1,0) facing east with delay set to 2 ticks. The repeater slows the signal before it reaches the torch's base. Right-click to change the delay — 1 tick gives the shortest pulses, 4 ticks the longest.

steps.title: Torch and output lamp
steps.explanation: Dust at (4,1,0) carries the delayed signal onto stone (4,0,0). A wall torch at (5,0,0) facing east is attached to that stone — it is ON normally and goes OFF only while stone (4,0,0) is powered. The lamp at (6,0,0) shows the torch output. Flip the lever ON then OFF quickly: the lamp will pulse briefly based on your repeater delay.
